applied_optimizations.name: Forward conv equivalence
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced conv_transpose2d with equivalent conv2d using flipped/transposed weights (w.flip(2,3).permute(1,0,2,3)). Pre-computed weight transformation. Achieves 2.46ms vs 2.44ms baseline.

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.error: ConvTranspose2d stores weight as (in_channels, out_channels, kh, kw), not (out_channels, in_channels, kh, kw). Multiple attempts to fix indexing still produced incorrect results.

attempted_but_failed.name: FP16 computation
attempted_but_failed.error: Benchmark reinitializes model weights, causing dtype mismatch between FP16 input and FP32 weight

notes: Baseline: 2.44ms for PyTorch conv_transpose2d on MI355X
Forward conv equivalence: 2.46ms (within noise of baseline)
stride=(1,1) makes conv_transpose2d equivalent to forward conv with flipped weights
Problem 78: 2D transposed conv, kernel=(3,7), stride=(1,1), padding=(1,3)
Operation is already well-optimized by PyTorch ROCm backend

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_6
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has been stuck for 5 consecutive trials (trials 2-6) with no output on a problem that is 95% GEMM-bound. The best score of 50 (correct but not faster than rocBLAS baseline of 2.44ms) is the real

global_notes: conv_transpose2d with stride=1 is well-optimized by rocBLAS
Custom Triton kernel would need significant debugging to match correctness
FP16 approach blocked by benchmark weight reinitialization
